headline: India first-baseline confirmation. Composite 34.4 sustained. HRW World Report 2026 documents post-2025 conflict aftermath: mass expulsions of Bengali-speaking Muslims from BJP-run states (1,500+ expelled to Bangladesh in May-June 2025 alone) without due process, sharp increase in hate speech, and suppression of dissent through media blocking and case-filing against academics. HRCP characterized Indian airstrikes as 'potential crimes against humanity.' Situation has stabilized post-ceasefire but documented pattern remains. No structural change in window. Sub-threshold confirmation; never-assessed entity establishes baseline. Re-queue scheduled June 4.
key_evidence: HRW World Report 2026: post-April-May 2025 India-Pakistan conflict, Indian authorities conducted mass expulsions of Bengali-speaking Muslims from BJP-run states to Bangladesh without due process; 1,500+ expelled in May-June 2025 alone.
HRW: hate speech against Muslims increased sharply during and after the conflict period.
HRW: authorities suppressed dissent by blocking media outlets and filing cases against academics critical of government conduct.
Human Rights Commission of Pakistan (HRCP) characterized Indian airstrikes during the conflict as 'potential crimes against humanity.'
Conflict has stabilized post-ceasefire; structural human rights concerns remain documented but situation is not actively deteriorating in window.

Never previously assessed — first-baseline establishment with EQU at 2.0 reflecting due-process gaps in expulsion process and AWR at 2.5 reflecting documented harm-recognition infrastructure within India's federated democracy.

evidence.finding: Documented patterns of Muslim expulsion, hate speech, dissent suppression, media blocking, and HRCP airstrike characterization.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 2.5 first-baseline. Federated democratic structure provides harm-recognition infrastructure; BJP-state-level documented patterns of selective recognition.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 2 first-baseline. Due-process gaps in mass expulsions of religious minority disproportionately affect specific communities. Floor-proximate.
scoring_rationale.BND: 2.5 first-baseline. Cross-border airstrike conduct documented but in conflict-context (verdict pending HRCP characterization).
scoring_rationale.ACC: 2.5 first-baseline. Media blocking and academic case-filing reflect accountability mechanism friction.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 2.5 first-baseline. Functional democracy at federal level; state-level governance variation creates asymmetric structure.
scoring_rationale.INT: 2 first-baseline. Public commitments to constitutional minority protections contradicted by documented expulsion conduct.

math_check.validator_check: First-baseline confirmation; zero delta; Developing band sustained.

negativeSignals: Mass expulsions of Bengali-speaking Muslims; sharp increase in hate speech; media blocking; academic dissent suppression; HRCP 'crimes against humanity' characterization of airstrikes.
positiveSignals: Federated democratic structure preserved; ceasefire stabilization post-conflict; India remains engaged with international human rights mechanisms.
sector_context: India is the largest never-assessed Country index entity by population (1.4B). First-baseline establishment fills a major staleness gap. The post-2025 conflict aftermath documents structural EQU and INT concerns; routine cycle should re-queue every 30 days.
open_actions_resolved: HRW 2026 evidence integrated into score record
open_actions_carried: Watch: any UN treaty body review of India's conduct in 2025 conflict
Watch: BJP-state expulsion process — any escalation triggers EQU dock
Watch: hate speech monitoring as INT input
